unit: Unit 12: Everyday Life with Your Mac
title: Online Shopping and Banking
drDigitalIntro: Online banking lets you check balances, pay bills, and transfer money without visiting a branch. Use your bank's official app or website (type the URL yourself — never click email links). Online shopping (Amazon, local store websites) lets you order from home with home delivery. Always check for https:// and the padlock icon before entering card details. Keep your receipts and check your statements for unexpected charges.

playgroundTask.instructions: You want to check your bank balance online. Which site is your real bank?

items.label: chaseonline.com
items.preview: CLICK HERE — Your Chase account needs IMMEDIATE VERIFICATION. Enter your username, password, and 16-digit card number NOW to avoid suspension. Offer expires in 10 minutes!

items.label: chase.com
items.preview: 🔒 https://www.chase.com — Sign in to access your accounts, pay bills, view statements, and transfer money. Protected by 256-bit encryption.

playgroundTask.fakeExplanation: Real bank sites never ask for your card number on the login page. 'chaseonline.com' is not Chase's real domain — banks own their exact name, like chase.com or bankofamerica.com. Urgency and countdown timers are major scam red flags.

drDigitalSuccess: You spotted the fake bank site! Always type your bank's address yourself and never click links from emails claiming to be from your bank.
drDigitalHint: What is the actual web address? And does a real bank ever demand your card number with a 10-minute countdown?
